rag_rules: Place generated AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL architecture rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers when available, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers when available, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not place assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in implementation scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
